citations.narrative:
The FY 25 Annual Report submitted to USDA was reviewed. There were no exceptions to the provisions of the AWA
reported on the USDA Annual Report for FY 25; however, IACUC approved protocol 23-08-0005 includes an exception to
the provisions of the AWA under Subpart F Feeding for swine.
Per this Section, the annual report shall assure that the research facility is adhering to the standards and regulations
under the Act, and that exceptions to the standards and regulations be specified and explained by the principal
investigator and approved by the IACUC. A summary of all such IACUC-approved exceptions must be attached to the
facility’s annual report that includes a brief explanation of the exceptions, as well as the species and number of animals
affected. In addition, any departures from the provisions of title 9, chapter I, subchapter A – Animal Welfare must be
specifically identified, along with the reasons for each departure, on the semi-annual reports sent by the IACUC to the
Institutional Official.
Correct by (1) submitting a corrected FY 25 Annual Report to USDA by 5/1/26 that includes a summary of the exception
with number of animals affected and (2) including any IACUC approved departures on future USDA annual reports from
this date 3/26/26 forward.

Four non-human primates (NHPs) were not handled as carefully as possible by veterinary technical staff. On 10/6/25, four
NHPs scheduled for routine semi-annual TB testing were mistakenly administered a tetanus vaccine instead of tuberculin.
The error occurred when veterinary technical staff preparing for the procedure selected the wrong bottle and did not verify
the label before withdrawing the doses. The veterinary technicians reported the error to the veterinarian who immediately
examined the NHPs and determined that the amount administered was small and consistent with a therapeutic dosing of
tetanus vaccine. No adverse effects from the vaccine were observed in the NHPs.
Per this Section, the handling of animals by all personnel should be done as carefully as possible in a manner that does
not cause physical harm or unnecessary discomfort. The four NHPs were not handled as carefully as possible during the
preventative medical procedure when veterinary technical staff failed to verify the medication vial label when withdrawing
the doses of medication and subsequently administered the incorrect product to the NHPs. Corrective measures were
implemented prior to this inspection, which included retraining all personnel involved in TB testing on all procedures to be
followed during testing, and the TB testing form was updated to include notation of the tuberculin lot number and to
include verification initials when doses are withdrawn. No additional incidents have occurred. To remain corrected from
this date 3/26/26.
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representatives.
*END OF REPORT*
n

narrative:
2.36(b)(3) 
   
Annual report. 
The FY 25 Annual Report submitted to USDA was reviewed. There were no exceptions to the provisions of the AWA 
reported on the USDA Annual Report for FY 25; however, IACUC approved protocol 23-08-0005 includes an exception to 
the provisions of the AWA under Subpart F Feeding for swine.  
 
Per this Section, the annual report shall assure that the research facility is adhering to the standards and regulations 
under the Act, and that exceptions to the standards and regulations be specified and explained by the principal 
investigator and approved by the IACUC. A summary of all such IACUC-approved exceptions must be attached to the 
facility’s annual report that includes a brief explanation of the exceptions, as well as the species and number of animals 
affected. In addition, any departures from the provisions of title 9, chapter I, subchapter A – Animal Welfare must be 
specifically identified, along with the reasons for each departure, on the semi-annual reports sent by the IACUC to the 
Institutional Official.  
 
Correct by (1) submitting a corrected FY 25 Annual Report to USDA by 5/1/26 that includes a summary of the exception 
with number of animals affected and (2) including any IACUC approved departures on future USDA annual reports from 
this date 3/26/26 forward. 
2.38(f)(1) 
   
Miscellaneous. 
Four non-human primates (NHPs) were not handled as carefully as possible by veterinary technical staff. On 10/6/25, four 
NHPs scheduled for routine semi-annual TB testing were mistakenly administered a tetanus vaccine instead of tuberculin. 
The error occurred when veterinary technical staff preparing for the procedure selected the wrong bottle and did not verify 
the label before withdrawing the doses. The veterinary technicians reported the error to the veterinarian who immediately 
examined the NHPs and determined that the amount administered was small and consistent with a therapeutic dosing of 
tetanus vaccine. No adverse effects from the vaccine were observed in the NHPs. 
 
Per this Section, the handling of animals by all personnel should be done as carefully as possible in a manner that does 
not cause physical harm or unnecessary discomfort. The four NHPs were not handled as carefully as possible during the 
preventative medical procedure when veterinary technical staff failed to verify the medication vial label when withdrawing

the doses of medication and subsequently administered the incorrect product to the NHPs. Corrective measures were 
implemented prior to this inspection, which included retraining all personnel involved in TB testing on all procedures to be 
followed during testing, and the TB testing form was updated to include notation of the tuberculin lot number and to 
include verification initials when doses are withdrawn. No additional incidents have occurred. To remain corrected from 
this date 3/26/26. 
 
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representatives.  
 
*END OF REPORT*
